life_history: Ian has a history of chronic depression that began in late adolescence, leading to feelings of isolation and frustration in social interactions. A recent breakup has triggered a relapse into depressive symptoms, compounding his struggles with mental health. He has been adjusting his SSRI medication, which has added to his stress. Ian often feels misunderstood by friends and struggles to connect, leading to further withdrawal and dissatisfaction in relationships.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am incompetent. I am helpless. I am powerless, weak, vulnerable. I am trapped.
Unlovable: I am unlovable. I am undesirable, unwanted. I am bound to be alone.
intermediate_beliefs: If I can't manage my own feelings or make others understand me, then I'm failing. I should be able to handle my mental health better.
When I'm depressed, nothing I do seems to make a difference. It feels like I'll never get better no matter what medication or strategies I try.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: If I can't even do simple things, it means I am failing.
Nobody likes me, and I am destined to be alone.
coping_strategies: Attempts to connect with friends for support, although these interactions often lead to frustration.
Uses distraction techniques like watching TV, reading, and sewing, though these have been less effective lately.

cognitive_models.situation: Trying to have a supportive conversation with a friend (Jamie) about mental health struggles.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: She doesn't understand me and is treating me like a patient rather than a friend. Nobody likes me.
cognitive_models.behavior: Ends the conversation prematurely and feels dissatisfied with the interaction. Withdraws from further social interactions.

cognitive_models.situation: Planned to have a productive weekend sewing and doing some catch-up work tasks.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I know how to do these things, It shouldn't be this hard. Why can't I even do such simple things?
cognitive_models.emotion: angry, mad, irritated, annoyed, disappointed, guilty
cognitive_models.behavior: Struggles to concentrate and becomes upset with the inability to perform familiar tasks, ultimately ended up watching TV all day.
